assumptions: The issue is the accounting in consolidated financial statements for foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend receivable.
The question asks for IFRS hedge accounting models potentially relevant to that foreign exchange exposure.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a recognised asset or liability as a possible hedged item, which makes the fair value hedge model a distinct top-level hedge accounting approach relevant to an FX exposure on a receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies forecast transactions as possible hedged items, including highly probable forecast intragroup transactions in limited cases in consolidated financial statements, so cash flow hedge accounting is a separate relevant model.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedged item and provide a separate hedge accounting model for foreign exchange risk.
